messages: What are sensible first steps to migrate old discs, CDs, or proprietary file formats into current, accessible digital formats?
Any recommended simple deduplication strategies when you have thousands of scanned images and possible repeats?
Given ransomware risk, what's the simplest offline backup workflow that balances safety and convenience?
When scanning a large box of papers, how do people minimize accidental duplicate scans?
If I sort files by name in a folder, will that reflect chronological order under my current naming scheme, or should alphabetical lists be considered misleading for time order?
Someone suggested switching the date element so the year comes first to make sorting more intuitive — would that be harmless to implement, or does it conflict with the way I currently organize files?
If I choose to adopt a year-first format, what's the least destructive batch approach that preserves the key identifying parts of each filename?
A physical document overlaps two categories; under my established physical filing approach, is it better to produce a second physical copy or to record a pointer to the single original?
Which of those strategies tends to be more maintainable over many years while still making retrieval straightforward?
On the digital side, would partitioning top-level directories according to how filenames are structured help searches, or is a single repository with a solid index preferable?
If an app wants to name incoming scans its own way, should I force names at import or allow the app then normalize the names with a script afterward?
When exporting a CSV index for someone else, which three columns are essential so they can deduce both category and approximate date without opening files?
If I need to find a document from early 2016 quickly, what pattern-based search would likely be fastest given my current filename construction?
For yearly archive bundles, is it better to mirror my usual filename construction or to use a year-first name so archives list in calendar order?
What's the simplest metadata tag or short code I can add to scans so someone can tell which physical container holds the original without altering visible filenames?
Finally, give me a concise checklist of steps to migrate old filenames to a year-first scheme while keeping a reversible record that links each digital file to its physical original.
